ticket.subject: Manifest questions
ticket.description:
Good morning,

 

The below issue happens a lot and I cannot for the life of me figure out why. Hoping you can assist!

 

I only “Fulfilled” 2 orders today (Jan.14th), but the plugin doesn’t show these orders on a printable manifest. I see a printable manifest from yesterday, but these orders were not fulfilled until this morning, so they should not have been on yesterday’s manifest.

 

Can you help me make sense of why this happens? Screenshot attached.

 

Orders fulfilled Jan. 14th with no manifest related:

#124961

#124913

The labels for these orders were generated yesterday, but not shipped until today.

 

 

Thanks,

 

Victoria Groetelaars

Greaves Jams & Marmalades, Box 26, Niagara on the Lake, ON, Canada L0S 1J0

www.greavesjams.com (http://www.greavesjams.com/)   1-905-468-3608  1-800-515-9939

comments.body:
Hello Victoria,

Good Day!

I would need to inform you about the process of Canada Post manifest generation. Once a label is generated for an order, the carrier manifest for that order will be generated in immediate next fulfilment batch generated.

Considering the recent fulfilment batch orders 124913 and 124961, the label was generated for the order 124913 on 10th January and for the order 124961 on 12th January.

The carrier manifest for the order 124913 was generated in the first fulfilment batch of 12th January and the carrier manifest for the order 124961 was generated in the second fulfilment batch of 12th January (highlighted in screenshot shared below).

You can download those carrier manifests and check once.

Hope I was able to clarify this.

 ![](https://pluginhive.zendesk.com/attachments/token/O5Se1rsF0uwQPaWe4sII1DsH5/?name=image.png)

Thanks and Regards,
Sagar K S
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Good morning,



I understand that the label will be on the next manifest after label generation, however this is confusing for the fulfillment processes.



I would request that your process gets updated, otherwise the entire “Mark As Fulfilled” status change button doesn’t seem to need to be there. It seems redundant or pointless then if this is already being fulfilled from any manifest generated.



I will give you an example; my team needs time to get the order together before it actually gets shipped out. The order needs to be packaged, and the fulfillment button is to mark it is ready to ship. This is our process:

1. Print order paperwork, generate label
2. Set aside to be packed by packing team (order is held until ready, most of the time it is completed the same day, but not every time)
3. Once paperwork comes back, we mark fulfilled



See how there should be a gap in the fulfillment because we still need to pack the order? Your current process doesn’t make any sense. Label generation cannot be the last step.



Please clarify and add in my request for an update to the Plugin.



Thanks,



![colour logo](https://support.pluginhive.com/attachments/token/X7w37MGdFnc4b3GFYrssWGIyD/?name=image003.jpg)

Victoria Groetelaars

Greaves Jams & Marmalades, Box 26, Niagara on the Lake, ON, Canada L0S 1J0

www.greavesjams.com  1-905-468-3608  1-800-515-9939

comments.body:
Hello Victoria,

We understand your concern and the order process flow.

Once the order fulfilment is initiated, we request the Canada Post for manifest generation. It is the carrier who will be generating the manifest and the process of manifest generation mentioned in the previous response is handled by the carrier and not from our end as we only request the carrier for the manifest generation.

So, for any clarifications on the process, you can check with the Canada Post team.

Thanks and Regards,
Sagar K S
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Good morning,



The previous plugin we used, I believe, was **Canada Post Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label.**



The issue seems to be a much bigger problem than you may realize. As we need to create the label before we pack the order, sometimes it won’t be ready to ship yet. If that order does not ship the same day as the label is generated, you’re telling me the manifest will be including this shipment without us marking it “fulfilled”.  This messes with workflow drastically and _creates an unreliable manifest_—a problem for us and the courier. The courier informed me (Canada Post) that they need the manifest to quickly scan all available packages that day, but then the manifest is not an accurate representation of the orders moving with the courier.



Moreover, Canada Post has previously advised us that labels cannot be cancelled anymore. So, if we need to redo a label (example a product is out of stock and we didn’t realize until packing), but it is a different day that we created the label on, we cannot use your system to cancel the label and regenerate a new one because your system is saying it’s already been shipped. Then, I am prevented from using the Shipping Plugin at all and I need to ship the order outside of the plugin, making everything manual. Also, if the packages were scanned automatically by Canada Post from the unreliable manifest provided from your plugin, their courier system will show the parcel was picked up and takes an extraordinary long time for Canada Post to sort out that the parcel was never picked up in the first place. The plugin basically created a “ghost package” and causes lots of grief.



If you’re saying this is all working as intended, my question then is, what is the point of marking anything “fulfilled”? How can we get this to work with our process as I have explained in detail? How do other companies do this with your plugin, or have you had other complaints about this issue? To me, this is not how fulfillment is supposed to work.



I do find the plugin unreliable honestly, has a ton of issues that are not compatible with our business, and it is taking more time to work through issues than expected when we installed the plugin.



Kindly review my answers and let me know if the process on PluginHive’s side will be updated to match my current workflow. Or please let me how to get this working better for my business.



Thank you,



![colour logo](https://support.pluginhive.com/attachments/token/Xk3PAqpp9PDrGLcoW4HNnwSBe/?name=image003.jpg)



Victoria Groetelaars

Greaves Jams & Marmalades, Box 26, Niagara on the Lake, ON, Canada L0S 1J0

www.greavesjams.com  1-905-468-3608  1-800-515-9939

Hello Victoria,

I have gone through your second query related to label cancelation. We rely on the Canada Post API for the label cancelation. Currently, we can see that your Canada Post account is a Contract account and for this type of account, the label cancelation is possible to our knowledge.
So, if Canada Post is not allowing for your account for label cancelation through their API, then the label cancelation is not possible through our plugin as well.

Regarding the Manifest generation and fulfilment related query, we will get back to you.

Thanks and Regards,
Sagar K S
Team PluginHive

comments.body:
Hi Sagar,



Thanks for that explanation. Canada Post does not allow for cancelled labels anymore and there is a reason for that, but it causes us more challenges.



Here is a direct message from Canada Post’s chat bot regarding cancelling labels:

” _If your shipping label was created **on or after July 2, 2025**:_

_You can simply discard any unused labels. You don't need to cancel your order as charges for these labels only occur after shipment induction (when the package is officially accepted by us).”_



The problem I face is I cannot use your Shipping plugin to generate a new label even if I need to discard of the old label. We get an error when trying to cancel and probably due to their API connection, and I understand that now since they don’t allow for labels to be cancelled. Still the problem, however, is that we cannot create a new label the shipment if the old one is invalid. That said, I **don’t** want to be able to create 2 shipping labels for the order because this could cause a duplicate order to be shipped potentially. What I do want is for the label which is “old” to be considered “not to ship” somehow, or mark label as “invalid” so I can generate a new one, or a different cancellation feature which allows me to discard the old label and still generate a new one without going outside the plugin.



A solution for this, along with the remaining manifest question I have, would be appreciated.



Thank you,



![colour logo](https://support.pluginhive.com/attachments/token/zCIxwkIpTRiC5TYT4XgqTZyzr/?name=image002.jpg)



Victoria Groetelaars

Greaves Jams & Marmalades, Box 26, Niagara on the Lake, ON, Canada L0S 1J0

www.greavesjams.com  1-905-468-3608  1-800-515-9939

Need to check with the team.

The customer is facing some difficulties in the process of Canada Post manifest generation through our WSS plugin.

Customer Case -
The customer has generated label for an order 123 on lets say 12th January and have fulfilled the order on 16th January. The current process involved in our WSS plugin is that when the customer fulfills the order 123 on 16th January, the carrier manifest is generated for that order and details are included in the next immediate manifest generated on 13th.

I have recorded a video showing the current process and have attached the recording for reference.

This does not align with customer's current order processing flow that is mentioned below.
Print order paperwork, generate label
Set aside to be packed by packing team (order is held until ready, most of the time it is completed the same day, but not every time)
Once paperwork comes back, we mark fulfilled

Customer is facing difficulties with the current manifestation process handled in our WSS plugin. I understand that the manifest are generated by Canada Post through their API but this process is not suitable for their process flow.
The customer also mentioned that this issue is not present in our "Canada Post Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label" and the process is what align their requirement. However, i am not sure of the process of manifest generation in that plugin and what steps differ from that plugin to our WSS plugin.

Here is the customer response "_The issue seems to be a much bigger problem than you may realize. As we need to create the label before we pack the order, sometimes it won’t be ready to ship yet. If that order does not ship the same day as the label is generated, you’re telling me the manifest will be including this shipment without us marking it “fulfilled”. This messes with workflow drastically and creates an unreliable manifest—a problem for us and the courier. The courier informed me (Canada Post) that they need the manifest to quickly scan all available packages that day, but then the manifest is not an accurate representation of the orders moving with the courier._
_Also, if the packages were scanned automatically by Canada Post from the unreliable manifest provided from your plugin, their courier system will show the parcel was picked up and takes an extraordinary long time for Canada Post to sort out that the parcel was never picked up in the first place. The plugin basically created a “ghost package” and causes lots of grief_".

So, is it possible to check about this case in detail and provide any update?

Store Details -
Canada store
46 orders in last 14 days
Canada Post and Canpar accounts added
61 labels in Jan
Active from Sept 2025 and Yearly Popular plan selected

comments.body:
Hello Victoria,

Good Day!

We have an update on your requirement.

When an order is fulfilled in the plugin, for all the manifest pending orders along with the current order, the carrier will generate a manifest and all those orders will be marked as shipped in the plugin.

The Canada Post manifest generation works on group of orders rather based on a single order.

If the orders are marked as Fulfilled individually, it results in multiple manifests getting created.

Considering your requirement, we will be bringing in an option to fulfill and transmit only selected orders for manifest generation. This enhancement would take sometime and we currently do not have an ETA for this.

So, for time being, is it possible for you to generate labels for the orders only when they are ready to ship, so the manifest will be generated as per your requirement. Please give this a try for some orders and check the working. Let us know how it goes.

Thanks and Regards,
Sagar K S
Team PluginHive

For your requirement, you can give a try by following the below mentioned steps which might work.

If the label is generated for an order and it is already fulfilled, then you can follow these steps.


·        First, change the status of the order in WooCommerce from Completed to Processing

·        In the Plugin, you will be seeing a red dot next to the order number

·        Select that order -> Click on Three Dots -> Cancel Shipment. This will cancel the fulfilment of the order in the plugin and clear the label. You can request a refund for the label directly from the Canada Post

·        Check if the status of the order in the plugin is in Processing and generate a new label with any updated details


Could you give this a try for an order and check if it works.

Hi Sagar,



Thanks for that explanation. Canada Post does not allow for cancelled labels anymore and there is a reason for that, but it causes us more challenges.



Here is a direct message from Canada Post’s chat bot regarding cancelling labels:

” _If your shipping label was created **on or after July 2, 2025**:_

_You can simply discard any unused labels. You don't need to cancel your order as charges for these labels only occur after shipment induction (when the package is officially accepted by us).”_



The problem I face is I cannot use your Shipping plugin to generate a new label even if I need to discard of the old label. We get an error when trying to cancel and probably due to their API connection, and I understand that now since they don’t allow for labels to be cancelled. Still the problem, however, is that we cannot create a new label the shipment if the old one is invalid. That said, I **don’t** want to be able to create 2 shipping labels for the order because this could cause a duplicate order to be shipped potentially. What I do want is for the label which is “old” to be considered “not to ship” somehow, or mark label as “invalid” so I can generate a new one, or a different cancellation feature which allows me to discard the old label and still generate a new one without going outside the plugin.



A solution for this, along with the remaining manifest question I have, would be appreciated.



Thank you,

Good morning,



The previous plugin we used, I believe, was **Canada Post Shipping Plugin for WooCommerce with Print Label.**



The issue seems to be a much bigger problem than you may realize. As we need to create the label before we pack the order, sometimes it won’t be ready to ship yet. If that order does not ship the same day as the label is generated, you’re telling me the manifest will be including this shipment without us marking it “fulfilled”.  This messes with workflow drastically and _creates an unreliable manifest_—a problem for us and the courier. The courier informed me (Canada Post) that they need the manifest to quickly scan all available packages that day, but then the manifest is not an accurate representation of the orders moving with the courier.



Moreover, Canada Post has previously advised us that labels cannot be cancelled anymore. So, if we need to redo a label (example a product is out of stock and we didn’t realize until packing), but it is a different day that we created the label on, we cannot use your system to cancel the label and regenerate a new one because your system is saying it’s already been shipped. Then, I am prevented from using the Shipping Plugin at all and I need to ship the order outside of the plugin, making everything manual. Also, if the packages were scanned automatically by Canada Post from the unreliable manifest provided from your plugin, their courier system will show the parcel was picked up and takes an extraordinary long time for Canada Post to sort out that the parcel was never picked up in the first place. The plugin basically created a “ghost package” and causes lots of grief.



If you’re saying this is all working as intended, my question then is, what is the point of marking anything “fulfilled”? How can we get this to work with our process as I have explained in detail? How do other companies do this with your plugin, or have you had other complaints about this issue? To me, this is not how fulfillment is supposed to work.



I do find the plugin unreliable honestly, has a ton of issues that are not compatible with our business, and it is taking more time to work through issues than expected when we installed the plugin.



Kindly review my answers and let me know if the process on PluginHive’s side will be updated to match my current workflow. Or please let me how to get this working better for my business.



Thank you,

comments.body:
Hello Victoria,

These steps will work if the order is marked as **Fulfilled** and the WooCommerce order is moved to **Completed** status. You may please give this a try.

In your current case, the order is in **Label Created** status. If you are okay with marking the order as Fulfilled and completing the WooCommerce order with the tracking number, you may proceed accordingly.

Once completed, you can change the WooCommerce order status back to **Processing**. This will reset the order status in the plugin as well and allow you to generate a new label.

Please note that the previously generated label may still remain active. In that case, you will need to contact Canada Post directly to have the label cancelled.

Please let us know if you need any assistance from our end.

Thanks and Regards,
Manohar V
Team PluginHive

These steps will work if the order is marked as Fulfilled and the WooCommerce order is moved to Completed status. You may please give this a try.
 
In your current case, the order is in Label Created status. If you are okay with marking the order as Fulfilled and completing the WooCommerce order with the tracking number, you may proceed accordingly.
 
Once completed, you can change the WooCommerce order status back to Processing. This will reset the order status in the plugin as well and allow you to generate a new label.
 
Please note that the previously generated label may still remain active. In that case, you will need to contact Canada Post directly to have the label cancelled.
 
Please let us know if you need any assistance from our end.
